summary: Open GM decompilation PR claiming exact-match work across 7 GM source files, with metadata also showing small ground.c/ground.h signature edits. The bot report records 9 newly matched functions and 15 additional unmatched-item improvements, including GM result, main GM, classic/training-mode GM, register-clear, and 1BA8 work.

key_files.role: GM matching work; diff shows changes in gm_8016247C, gm_80164910, gm_80166378, fn_8016719C, and gm_8016A164; bot reports new matches for fn_8016719C, gm_80164910, and gm_8016A164 plus improvements for gm_80166378 and gm_8016247C.

diff_excerpt: gm_80166378 pointer-iteration rewritten to indexed player_standings access with PAD_STACK(56)
diff_excerpt: gm_8016A164 gp pointer and PAD_STACK(4)

what_changed: PR body states exact-match decompilation of 7 GM files with 9 newly matched functions and runner-validated exact matches.
Bot report records matched code increasing to 72.29% (+0.15%, +5964 bytes), with 9 new matches and 15 improvements in unmatched items.

gm_1601.c changed several match-sensitive source shapes: narrowed internal_id to u8 in gm_80164910, rewrote gm_80166378 from pointer-walk locals to direct indexed player_standings accesses, added PAD_STACK(56), changed flag tests and a facing-direction if/else to a ternary in fn_8016719C, and added a global pointer/empty null-check shape in gm_8016A164.

gm_1832.c changed local declaration/lifetime shape, added an explicit case 0 break, cached lbl_804735E8.xE4 in kind, introduced a typedef for lbl_804736B0 static state, and used extern float constants in fn_80186EFC and fn_80188644.
Changed-file metadata includes small edits in ground.c and ground.h, but the loaded diff excerpt does not show their exact content.

smart_moves.text: The PR body and bot report both preserve narrow validation evidence: production baseline 0b15e71345, ninja changes_all, clean regression report, and a per-function match/improvement table.

For fn_8016719C, the diff uses a typed bitfield view for match_info->FighterMatchInfo[slot].xC instead of repeated raw shift/mask temporaries, while keeping the local struct scoped to the function area.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: struct fn_8016719C_xC_bits and temp_r4 use

smart_moves.text: For fn_80184138, the repeated lbl_804735E8.xE4 comparison was factored into a local kind variable, reducing repeated casts while preserving branch decisions shown in the diff.

decomp_lessons.lesson: Direct indexed array access can be the matching shape even when pointer-walk locals look cleaner. In gm_80166378, the diff removes MatchPlayerData* p/q and kills-pointer temporaries and writes repeated arg0->player_standings[i/j] expressions; the bot report records a large improvement for that function.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: gm_80166378 pointer loop rewritten to indexed player_standings access

decomp_lessons.lesson: Small signedness and width changes can close near-matches. The diff narrows gm_80164910 internal_id from s32 to u8 and the bot report records gm_80164910 reaching 100%.

Match-sensitive control flow may prefer ordinary source forms such as an explicit switch case or a ternary. The diff adds case 0: break in fn_8018325C and replaces a facing-direction if/else with a ternary in fn_8016719C; both functions are reported as newly matched or improved.
evidence_refs: text_corpus issue_comment: fn_8018325C and fn_8016719C new matches

decomp_lessons.lesson: Some exact matches in this PR rely on narrow codegen steering such as PAD_STACK, local lifetime/order changes, extern float constants, and pointer aliases to globals. These are supported here by the PR's verification text and bot report, but should remain narrow tactics rather than general style.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: gp = &lbl_8046B488 with empty null-check shape

naming_conventions.convention: File-local recovered static-data structs can use path/address-derived names when semantics are not fully proven, as shown by gm_1832_804736B0_t for lbl_804736B0.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: typedef struct gm_1832_804736B0_t ... static gm_1832_804736B0_t lbl_804736B0

naming_conventions.convention: Short role names are used where local role is evident, such as kind for lbl_804735E8.xE4, data for &lbl_804736B0, and gp for &lbl_8046B488.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: struct lbl_8046B488_t* gp in gm_8016A164

naming_conventions.convention: Unknown global/data symbols remain address-style names such as lbl_804DA5C8 and lbl_804DA60C rather than speculative semantic names.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use PAD_STACK only where needed for exact stack shape; this PR adds PAD_STACK(56) in gm_80166378 and smaller PAD_STACK uses in gm_8016A164/fn_80186EFC.
Move declarations to alter local lifetime/stack allocation when exact matching requires it, as shown by moving Vec3 scale inside the entity2 block and hoisting HSD_JObj* jobj2 in fn_8018325C.
Use unsigned casts and literal suffixes to steer compare/divide codegen, as seen in gm_8016247C casts to u32 and gm_80166378 divisions by 60U.
evidence_refs: text_corpus issue_comment: gm_8016247C and gm_80166378 improvements

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Extern float constants can be introduced for literal/data relocation matching, but the evidence here only shows their use and resulting report entries, not independent data ownership proof.
A temporary bitfield overlay can express flag tests/sets and affect bit access codegen, shown by fn_8016719C_xC_bits over FighterMatchInfo[slot].xC.

PR state is OPEN and merged_at is null in the loaded metadata; no merge or acceptance status is inferred.
No human review comments are loaded; review feedback is limited to the decomp-dev bot report.
The PR body claims runner validation and clean regression reports; this intake records that claim as PR evidence and does not independently rerun builds or objdiff.
Extern float constants and small ground header/source edits may represent data/prototype work, but loaded evidence is insufficient to promote a source-owned fact.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Indexed array expressions can outperform pointer-walk temporaries for MWCC matching
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR 2656, gm_80166378 moved from MatchPlayerData* pointer-walk locals to repeated arg0->player_standings[i/j] indexing and improved from 90.13% to 98.43% in the bot report.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: gm_80166378 pointer locals removed and indexed expressions added

accepted_candidate_records.title: Narrow local type changes can close GM near-matches
accepted_candidate_records.text: gm_80164910 changed internal_id from s32 to u8 and is listed as a new 100% match in the bot report.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Keep codegen-steering tactics narrow and verifier-backed
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2656 uses tactics such as PAD_STACK, declaration movement, extern float constants, typed bitfield overlays, and local global pointers. The PR body and bot report provide validation evidence for this slice, but the tactics should not be generalized without local match evidence.

source_update_candidates.title: Document bitfield overlays as a typed-field matching tactic when real packed flag ownership is not fully recovered
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2656 shows a function-local bitfield struct over FighterMatchInfo[slot].xC in fn_8016719C replacing raw byte shift/mask temporaries. This may be a useful bridge tactic when packed flags are known enough to express typed bits but not yet recovered into the owning struct.
source_update_candidates.reason: Could refine existing standards around typed fields and bridge structs; curator should verify whether this pattern is acceptable beyond this PR.

source_update_candidates.title: Warn that extern float literal symbols in code-match PRs need data ownership evidence
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2656 introduces lbl_804DA5C8 and lbl_804DA60C extern float constants in gm_1832.c and the bot report lists a .sdata2 improvement. The loaded evidence does not prove data ownership, so this should be treated as a cautionary matching tactic requiring explicit section/symbol evidence.
source_update_candidates.reason: The PR evidence supports a possible source-standard note, but not an accepted data ownership fact.

source_update_candidates.title: GM decomp slice may require synchronized ground header/source prototype changes
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2656 changed src/melee/gr/ground.c and src/melee/gr/ground.h by one line each alongside GM matching work, but the loaded diff excerpt does not show the exact change.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential cross-path dependency for GM/stage work; curator should inspect full diff before recording any path fact.

rejection_notes: Do not record the PR as merged or accepted; loaded metadata says state OPEN and merged_at null.
Do not promote the extern float constants lbl_804DA5C8/lbl_804DA60C as proven data ownership; the excerpt only shows their use and a report entry.
Do not infer details of ground.c/ground.h changes from the one-line metadata alone.
Do not treat PAD_STACK, empty null-check blocks, or extern literal symbols as general style; this PR only supports them as narrow, verifier-backed matching tactics.
